heading: Environmental review criteria
label: 1505 Environmental review criteria
notes: References in TextThe National Environmental Policy Act, referred to in subsec. (a), probably means the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969, , , , which is classified generally to chapter 55 (§ 4321 et seq.) of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 91–19083 Stat. 852section 4321 of Title 42Jan. 1, 1970

children.text: the effect on the marine environment;
the effect on oceanographic currents and wave patterns;
the effect on alternate uses of the oceans and navigable waters, such as scientific study, fishing, and exploitation of other living and nonliving resources;
the potential dangers to a deepwater port from waves, winds, weather, and geological conditions, and the steps which can be taken to protect against or minimize such dangers;
effects of land-based developments related to deepwater port development;
the effect on human health and welfare; and
such other considerations as the Secretary deems necessary or appropriate.

children.heading: Review and revision
children.label: b Review and revision
children.text: The Secretary shall periodically review and, whenever necessary, revise in the same manner as originally developed, criteria established pursuant to subsection (a) of this section.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: c Requirement
children.text: The criteria established pursuant to this section shall be developed concurrently with the regulations promulgated pursuant to and in accordance with that section and in accordance with the provisions of that subsection.section 1504(a) of this title
